comment: Encoder ablation: same Guard pipeline (question-aware NLI + min_entail_votes=1) but with adaptmem's FT-300 tuned bi-encoder swapped in for generic all-MiniLM-L6-v2. Hypothesis: domain-tuned bi-encoder lifts cosine precision because lexically-similar but semantically-irrelevant chunks score lower. Result: no measurable lift on HaluEval QA — F1 identical to generic baseline at the best threshold (0.70). Likely because HaluEval QA gives a single 1-chunk corpus per case; tuned-encoder's disambiguation advantage requires larger corpora. Re-test on RAGTruth public mirror (when accessible) for span-level multi-chunk corpora.

delta_vs_generic.interpretation: Identical at the best threshold. HaluEval QA's 1-chunk-per-case corpus structure does not exercise the tuned encoder's disambiguation advantage; large multi-chunk corpora (RAGTruth) needed for a fair test of the hypothesis.
